title: About Me
text:
Hello!
I’m Jennifer Onyebuchi, a social impact leader and passionate advocate for digital inclusion and gender equality in technology. I’m currently pursuing my Master’s in Data Science at the University of British Columbia as a Mastercard Foundation Scholar, where I’m building the skills to become a data scientist.
My passion lies in harnessing the power of data to bridge inequities—especially in education, technology access, and opportunities for underrepresented groups. This passion inspired me to found the Tech Big Sis Foundation, where we empower African children and young girls with digital skills, preparing them for the future of work and breaking cycles of inequality.
Through initiatives like Tech Every African Child Early (TEACE) and the Raising Afro-Tech-Girls Movement (RAM), I’ve led programs that have reached hundreds of children and mentored dozens of young women, ensuring they are not left behind in the digital age.
Beyond academics and advocacy, I enjoy mentoring, public speaking, and creating innovative ways to use technology for social good. My journey is anchored in one belief: every child deserves the opportunity to thrive in the digital age, regardless of where they are born.
